Platform & Data.narrative: This family ensures your fleet system is accessible to drivers and managers anywhere, protects sensitive data under Singapore's PDPA rules, and connects seamlessly with your accounting and HR systems. SMEs need this foundation to keep operations running smoothly across devices and to comply with data protection regulations. For example, a delivery manager can check vehicle status on their phone while a driver logs trip details on a tablet, and all data syncs securely to your accounting system.

Vehicle Registry.narrative: This family maintains a single source of truth for every vehicle in your fleet—from registration details to insurance policies and claims history. SMEs benefit by having all vehicle information in one place, reducing paperwork and ensuring nothing falls through the cracks when managing insurance or handling accidents. For instance, when a vehicle is involved in an incident, you can instantly pull its insurance details and claims history to file a claim quickly.

Driver & Safety.narrative: This family tracks driver qualifications, monitors unsafe driving habits like speeding or harsh braking, and records accidents to reduce risk and insurance costs. SMEs should care because safer drivers mean fewer claims, lower premiums, and better compliance with employment and road safety rules. For example, if a driver consistently scores poorly on safety metrics, you can flag them for retraining before an accident happens.

Maintenance & Workshop.narrative: This family automates service reminders based on time or mileage and tracks all repairs and maintenance work orders in one place. SMEs benefit by preventing costly breakdowns, extending vehicle life, and keeping maintenance costs predictable. For example, the system reminds you when a vehicle is due for its 40,000 km service, and you can assign the work order to your preferred workshop and track progress in real time.

Operations & Logistics.narrative: This family tracks fuel consumption, vehicle location via GPS, trip details, and allows staff to book shared vehicles—giving you visibility into how your fleet is being used and where money is being spent. SMEs need this to spot inefficiencies, reduce fuel waste, and ensure vehicles are available when needed. For example, you can see that one vehicle is consuming more fuel than similar ones, or that a shared pool vehicle is booked 80% of the time, justifying the investment.

Cost & Compliance.narrative: This family calculates total cost of ownership per vehicle, generates reports on fleet performance, and tracks Singapore-specific compliance obligations like road tax, COE, and vehicle inspections. SMEs should care because understanding true fleet costs helps with budgeting and pricing, while staying on top of compliance avoids fines and vehicle downtime. For example, a dashboard shows you that Vehicle A costs 15% more per kilometre than Vehicle B, prompting a review of its maintenance or replacement.

Tyre Management.narrative: This family monitors tyre condition, schedules rotations, and tracks replacements to maximise tyre life and safety. While useful for larger fleets, SMEs with smaller fleets may manage this manually or through their workshop vendor.

Vendor & Workshop Management.narrative: This family maintains a register of external service providers, tracks their quality and turnaround times, and manages costs. SMEs with established relationships with one or two trusted workshops may not need formal vendor management in the system.

Route Planning & Optimisation.narrative: This family plans multi-stop delivery routes and optimises them for distance, time, or fuel cost. SMEs with simple, repetitive routes or small delivery areas may not need advanced route optimisation.

Pool Vehicle & Car Sharing.narrative: This family manages self-service booking, keyless access, and usage-based billing for shared vehicle pools. SMEs with a small pool or informal sharing arrangements may not need a dedicated system for this.

Environmental & Emissions Tracking.narrative: This family monitors fleet carbon emissions and tracks progress toward sustainability goals. SMEs focused on cost control may defer this unless they face customer or regulatory pressure to report emissions.

Fleet Analytics & AI.narrative: This family applies machine learning to predict maintenance needs and optimise replacement decisions. SMEs with stable, smaller fleets may not generate enough data to benefit from advanced analytics.

Fleet Procurement & Disposal.narrative: This family manages vehicle acquisition, lease versus buy decisions, and replacement planning based on total cost of ownership. SMEs that buy or lease vehicles infrequently may handle this outside the system.
